case_name: Douse v Western Sussex Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust [2019] EWHC 2294 (QB) (27 August 2019)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The Claimant, Hollie Douse, suffered a serious hypoxic ischaemic injury during the course of her birth by caesarean section on 28 th October 2012 which has led to severe and profound disability in relation to all aspects of functioning.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The issue for this court in the liability only trial has been whether or not the obstetric registrar, Dr Raykova, was negligent in failing to deliver Hollie within a significantly shorter period of time, and in particular within five minutes.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The expert evidence came from two consultant obstetricians, Mr Andrew Farkas on behalf of the Claimant, and Mr Derek Tuffnell for the Defendant.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr Farkas believes that a vaginal examination immediately before the caesarean procedure might have identified whether there had been further dilatation, which would have supported a further attempt at vaginal delivery.
evidence_index.speaker: Mr. Farkas

evidence_index.text: Mr Tuffnell believed that it was unreasonable to assume that birth of a baby by caesarean section can always be achieved within four minutes.
evidence_index.speaker: Mr. Tuffnell

evidence_index.text: It must follow that the Claimant succeeds on the issue of liability and causation, and is entitled to damages to be assessed at a future date.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: In my judgment, having concluded on a balance of probabilities that there was no material change in presentation between that faced by Dr Raykova and Dr Stone, the most likely explanation for the relative promptness of delivery for Dr Stone, which I accept was far more difficult than had been anticipated prior to the surgery, is that Dr Raykova was not possessed of the necessary skill and expertise to cope with such difficulties.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Mr Douse had been present throughout the labour and attended at theatre scrubbed up for the caesarean section. He was standing near to his partner's head.
evidence_index.speaker: Mr. Douse

evidence_index.text: The penultimate vaginal examination at 2300, before the foetal blood sample was taken, which I have referred to briefly above, is relevant, insofar as it informed the process that followed thereafter and has been the subject of some discussion in the trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The notes reveal that Dr Raykova was unable to deliver the baby despite trying a number of different methods including using the midwife to apply pressure through the vagina, tilting the bed to allow some gravitational pull, administration of terbutaline to relax the uterus and to reduce contractions.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: The patient, Vanya Jackson, was admitted due to recurring high blood pressure and was induced for labour due to hypertension concerns. She had gestational diabetes and a high BMI. Labour was induced with a propess pessary, and syntocinon was commenced after the application of an epidural.

requestables.name: Foetal Blood Sample
requestables.description: Measurement of acid levels in foetal blood

requestables.name: Consultant Obstetrician Note
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr Stone regarding delivery method

reveal.result_summary: Decision to proceed with caesarean section
reveal.clinical_significance: Consultant agreed with caesarean due to foetal distress

timeline_phases.name: Labour Induction
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted for induction due to hypertension

decision_points.prompt: What should be the approach to deliver the baby?

options.description: Proceed with caesarean section using standard techniques
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard techniques were insufficient due to impaction
Consultant assistance could have expedited delivery

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant attempted delivery using standard techniques

expected_action_court.description: Consultant should have been called immediately

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Delay in calling consultant led to prolonged delivery
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Consultant should have been involved sooner

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to use appropriate techniques
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented injury

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to use varied techniques during delivery
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Alternative techniques could have facilitated delivery

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted for induction due to hypertension
Initial delivery attempts by Dr Raykova

allegations.allegation: Failure to deliver within five minutes
Failure to use appropriate delivery techniques
